lotl.label: What's Going On With the Leafs
lotl.body: The Toronto Maple Leafs' season slammed shut like a rink door in a blizzard - another year of promise curdling into frustration, leaving fans to scrape the ice for answers. Their 32-36-14 ledger landed them 8th in the Atlantic, 35 games back with just 78 points, capped by a grim L7 streak that buried any faint playoff flicker. The core - Mitch Marner, Auston Matthews, William Nylander - carried the load again, but defensive lapses and goaltending woes turned elite offense into fool's gold; 15th in playoff seedings says it all. Whispers of coaching upheaval swirl around Sheldon Keefe's future, while free agency looms with cap-strapped choices on UFAs like a disgruntled Tavares. Draft lottery odds offer a sliver of hope - perhaps a top-10 pick to inject youth into this aging machine. The Marlies' uneven AHL grind mirrors the big club's talent pipeline issues. Watch the lottery draw and Shanahan's offseason purge - that's where Toronto rebuilds or reloads for 2026-27.

the_latest.headline: Why The NHL's 'Ball-By-Ball' Draft Lottery Will Be A Must-Watch For Maple Leafs Fans
NHLPA poll: Sidney Crosby most likely to become head coach
the_latest.dek: Sidney Crosby is seen by his peers as the NHL player most likely to become a head coach, according to the National Hockey League Players' Association's annual player poll released Friday.

the_latest.dek: The Avalanche and Lightning are tied for the longest active consecutive playoff streaks in the NHL. See which other teams make the list.

lotl.label: What's Going On With the Jays
lotl.body: Another grand slam catastrophe struck the Toronto Blue Jays last night in Arizona, where Corbin Carroll crushed Jeff Hoffman's offering to left-center in the eighth, flipping a 2-2 tie into a 6-2 gut punch that extended their misery to four straight losses. They're 7-13 now, dead last in the AL East at fifth place, 5 games back and hemorrhaging momentum after dropping both to the Brewers and now the Diamondbacks - outscored, outdueled, and out of excuses in a desert series that's exposed every flaw. Hoffman, who piled up 33 saves last year, has been a strikeout machine with 18 K's in under nine innings this season, but that bases-loaded bomb stranded Toronto's eight runners left on base despite 12 hits; the bats went 2-for-9 with men in scoring position, a familiar choke. No fresh injuries reported, but this slump screams deeper rot - the rotation's next, Kevin Gausman desperately toeing the rubber this afternoon for series salvation. Win today, or stare down a 7 - 13 abyss heading home - the AL East won't wait for wake-up calls.

lotl.label: What's Going On With the Commanders
lotl.body: The draft smoke thickens around Washington, where a 5-12 stumble left them third in the NFC East, nine games back, and armed with the 14th pick in a loaded class - perfect for flipping misery into muscle. Jack Sperry's perfect seven-round mock dropped Saturday, handing the Commanders Makai Lemon to juice Jayden Daniels' barren receiver corps beyond Terry McLaurin, then Jermod McCoy at corner to shore up a secondary that's been leaking like a sieve. Free agency pruned vets like Deebo Samuel, Austin Ekeler, and Von Miller off the roster, clearing cap for trenches and speed, while mocks whisper trade-downs - snag extra Day 2 picks from desperate Cowboys or Giants, maybe pouncing on Jeremiyah Love if he slides. Daniels, their franchise spark, needs weapons now; that 79-catch, 1,156-yard Biletnikoff shadow in mocks screams instant impact. No more band-aids - this front office eyes long-term: edge, OT, ILB like Harold Perkins Jr. to bury rivals. With the draft clock ticking next week, watch if they deal back for volume or swing big at No. 14 - roster rebirth or bust hangs on it.
